questions.text: What figure of speech is used in this sentence, "He is the black sheep of the class."
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: A simile is a figure of speech that directly compares two things.Example: I ran like the wind.

A Metaphor is a figure of speech in which a word or phrase is applied to an object or action to which it is not literally applicable. In the given sentence,a person is being compared to a black sheep, making it is a metaphor.
A Alliteration is the repetition of usually initial consonant sounds in two or more neighboring words or syllables.Example: Wild and wooly.
Hyperbole is a figure of speech that uses an exaggerated or extravagant statement to create a strong emotional response.Example: I am so hungry I could eat a horse.

questions.explanation: In the question, the person is being compared to something non-human form like sheep so it is a metaphor.
